rationale: The citing paper explicitly studies upfront-fee markets including online courses and models sunk-cost effects in contract design, while the cited paper directly identifies payment-induced sunk-cost and certificate effects on engagement in MOOCs. Given this close construct and context overlap (payment/upfront fees, sunk costs, online courses), the citation is plausibly substantive rather than an incidental mention, even though only DOI-level match evidence is available.
